title: Getting started with rkaf
text: rkaf provides Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks R users torch backend. package supports: regression binary classification multiclass classification formula matrix interfaces mini-batch training validation splits early stopping automatic standardization best-model restoration KAF-specific diagnostics vignette gives quick tour main workflow.
code: library(rkaf)  set.seed(123) torch::torch_manual_seed(123)

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: first fit KAF model synthetic one-dimensional function low-frequency high-frequency structure.
code: x <- as.matrix(seq(-1, 1, length.out = 128))  y <- sin(8 * pi * x) +   0.35 * cos(3 * pi * x) +   0.15 * x^2 fit <- kaf_fit(   x = x,   y = y,   hidden = c(256, 256),   num_grids = 32,   use_layernorm = FALSE,   epochs = 1000,   lr = 1e-3,   standardize_x = FALSE,   standardize_y = TRUE,   fourier_init_scale = 5e-2,   restore_best = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Architecture: 1 -> 256 -> 256 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 32  #> Epochs:       1000 #> Batch size:   128 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: no  #> Standardize y: yes  #> Final train loss: 0.0227977 #> Best train loss:  0.0198582 at epoch 945 pred <- predict(fit, x)  head(data.frame(   observed = round(as.numeric(y), 3),   predicted = round(pred, 3) )) #>   observed predicted #> 1   -0.200     0.195 #> 2    0.185     0.356 #> 3    0.517     0.474 #> 4    0.748     0.540 #> 5    0.842     0.545 #> 6    0.787     0.486 plot(   x,   y,   type = "l",   lwd = 2,   xlab = "x",   ylab = "f(x)",   main = "KAF regression fit" )  lines(x, pred, lwd = 2, lty = 2)  legend(   "topright",   legend = c("Observed", "Predicted"),   lty = c(1, 2),   lwd = 2,   bty = "n" )

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: tabular data, rkaf also supports formula interface.
code: fit_mtcars <- kaf_fit_formula(   mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl,   data = mtcars,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 16,   epochs = 200,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_mtcars #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Formula:      mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl #> Architecture: 3 -> 32 -> 32 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 16  #> Epochs:       200 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: yes  #> Final train loss: 0.0542788 #> Best train loss:  0.0542788 at epoch 200 mtcars_pred <- predict(fit_mtcars, mtcars)  head(data.frame(   observed = mtcars$mpg,   predicted = round(mtcars_pred, 2) )) #>   observed predicted #> 1     21.0     21.16 #> 2     21.0     21.15 #> 3     22.8     21.86 #> 4     21.4     20.03 #> 5     18.7     16.58 #> 6     18.1     18.30

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: response factor two classes, rkaf automatically treats problem binary classification. Predicted probabilities classes: Confusion matrix Raw logits:
code: df <- mtcars  df$high_mpg <- factor(   ifelse(df$mpg > median(df$mpg), "yes", "no"),   levels = c("no", "yes") ) fit_binary <- kaf_fit_formula(   high_mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl,   data = df,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 16,   epochs = 200,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_binary #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         binary #> Classes:      no, yes #> Formula:      high_mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl #> Architecture: 3 -> 32 -> 32 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 16  #> Epochs:       200 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final train loss: 0.00163561 #> Best train loss:  0.00163561 at epoch 200 prob_binary <- predict(fit_binary, df, type = "prob") class_binary <- predict(fit_binary, df, type = "class")  head(data.frame(   observed = df$high_mpg,   prob_yes = round(prob_binary, 3),   predicted = class_binary )) #>   observed prob_yes predicted #> 1      yes    0.999       yes #> 2      yes    0.999       yes #> 3      yes    1.000       yes #> 4      yes    0.983       yes #> 5       no    0.000        no #> 6       no    0.008        no table(   observed = df$high_mpg,   predicted = class_binary ) #>         predicted #> observed no yes #>      no  17   0 #>      yes  0  15 head(predict(fit_binary, df, type = "link")) #> [1]  7.551862  6.966256  7.803174  4.073124 -8.898857 -4.818394

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: response factor two classes, rkaf fits multiclass classifier. Confusion matrix Class probabilities:
code: fit_iris <- kaf_fit_formula(   Species ~ .,   data = iris,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 16,   epochs = 300,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_iris #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         multiclass #> Classes:      setosa, versicolor, virginica #> Formula:      Species ~ . #> Architecture: 4 -> 32 -> 32 -> 3 #> Fourier grids: 16  #> Epochs:       300 #> Batch size:   150 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final train loss: 0.00819381 #> Best train loss:  0.00819381 at epoch 300 class_iris <- predict(fit_iris, iris, type = "class")  table(   observed = iris$Species,   predicted = class_iris ) #>             predicted #> observed     setosa versicolor virginica #>   setosa         50          0         0 #>   versicolor      0         50         0 #>   virginica       0          0        50 head(round(predict(fit_iris, iris, type = "prob"), 3)) #>      setosa versicolor virginica #> [1,]  0.999      0.001     0.000 #> [2,]  0.995      0.003     0.002 #> [3,]  0.997      0.001     0.001 #> [4,]  0.997      0.001     0.001 #> [5,]  0.999      0.000     0.000 #> [6,]  1.000      0.000     0.000

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: kaf_fit() supports validation splits, mini-batches, early stopping. fitted object stores train_loss_history validation_loss_history, users can inspect training validation behavior directly.
code: fit_val <- kaf_fit(   x = x,   y = y,   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   use_layernorm = FALSE,   epochs = 300,   lr = 5e-4,   batch_size = 64,   validation_split = 0.2,   patience = 100,   restore_best = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  plot(fit_val)

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: KAF architecture contains base/GELU branch Fourier branch. package exposes helper functions inspect learned branch scales Fourier parameters.

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: Advanced users can use low-level torch modules directly.
code: model <- nn_kaf(   layers = c(4, 16, 16, 1),   num_grids = 8 )  x_tensor <- torch::torch_randn(10, 4) y_tensor <- model(x_tensor)  y_tensor$shape #> [1] 10  1

title: Getting started with rkaf
text: standard workflow : classification, use:
code: fit <- kaf_fit_formula(   y ~ .,   data = df,   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   validation_split = 0.2,   patience = 30 )  predict(fit, newdata) plot(fit) extract_kaf_scales(fit) predict(fit, newdata, type = "prob") predict(fit, newdata, type = "class")

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: rkaf R package Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks using torch backend. package provides modern R interface KAF models, including: regression binary classification multiclass classification formula matrix interfaces mini-batch training validation splits early stopping automatic standardization best-model restoration KAF-specific diagnostics goal rkaf make KAF-style neural networks accessible R users without requiring Python, reticulate, custom training loops.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: can install development version locally : local package directory: rkaf depends torch. torch already configured, run:
code: devtools::install() devtools::load_all() torch::install_torch()

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: example intentionally uses Fourier-heavy target function demonstrate model’s ability learn oscillatory structure.
code: library(rkaf)  set.seed(123) torch::torch_manual_seed(123)  x <- as.matrix(seq(-1, 1, length.out = 128))  y <- sin(8 * pi * x) +   0.35 * cos(3 * pi * x) +   0.15 * x^2  fit <- kaf_fit(   x = x,   y = y,   hidden = c(256, 256),   num_grids = 32,   use_layernorm = FALSE,   epochs = 1000,   lr = 1e-3,   standardize_x = FALSE,   standardize_y = TRUE,   fourier_init_scale = 5e-2,   restore_best = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Architecture: 1 -> 256 -> 256 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 32  #> Epochs:       1000 #> Batch size:   128 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: no  #> Standardize y: yes  #> Final train loss: 0.0227977 #> Best train loss:  0.0198582 at epoch 945 pred <- predict(fit, x)  plot(   x,   y,   type = "l",   lwd = 2,   xlab = "x",   ylab = "f(x)",   main = "KAF regression example" )  lines(x, pred, lwd = 2, lty = 2)  legend(   "topright",   legend = c("Observed", "Predicted"),   lty = c(1, 2),   lwd = 2,   bty = "n" ) plot(fit)

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: rkaf also supports formula interface tabular data.
code: fit_mtcars <- kaf_fit_formula(   mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl,   data = mtcars,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 16,   epochs = 200,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_mtcars #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         regression #> Formula:      mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl #> Architecture: 3 -> 32 -> 32 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 16  #> Epochs:       200 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: yes  #> Final train loss: 0.0542788 #> Best train loss:  0.0542788 at epoch 200 mtcars_pred <- predict(fit_mtcars, mtcars)  head(data.frame(   observed = mtcars$mpg,   predicted = round(mtcars_pred, 2) )) #>   observed predicted #> 1     21.0     21.16 #> 2     21.0     21.15 #> 3     22.8     21.86 #> 4     21.4     20.03 #> 5     18.7     16.58 #> 6     18.1     18.30

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: factor, character, logical targets two classes, rkaf can fit binary classifier automatically. Predicted probabilities class:
code: df <- mtcars df$high_mpg <- factor(   ifelse(df$mpg > median(df$mpg), "yes", "no"),   levels = c("no", "yes") )  fit_binary <- kaf_fit_formula(   high_mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl,   data = df,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 16,   epochs = 200,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_binary #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         binary #> Classes:      no, yes #> Formula:      high_mpg ~ wt + hp + cyl #> Architecture: 3 -> 32 -> 32 -> 1 #> Fourier grids: 16  #> Epochs:       200 #> Batch size:   32 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final train loss: 0.00163561 #> Best train loss:  0.00163561 at epoch 200 prob_binary <- predict(fit_binary, df, type = "prob") class_binary <- predict(fit_binary, df, type = "class")  head(data.frame(   observed = df$high_mpg,   prob_yes = round(prob_binary, 3),   predicted = class_binary )) #>   observed prob_yes predicted #> 1      yes    0.999       yes #> 2      yes    0.999       yes #> 3      yes    1.000       yes #> 4      yes    0.983       yes #> 5       no    0.000        no #> 6       no    0.008        no  table(   observed = df$high_mpg,   predicted = class_binary ) #>         predicted #> observed no yes #>      no  17   0 #>      yes  0  15

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: factor targets two classes, rkaf fits multiclass model. Class probabilities: Predicted classes:
code: fit_iris <- kaf_fit_formula(   Species ~ .,   data = iris,   hidden = c(32, 32),   num_grids = 16,   epochs = 300,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_iris #> <kaf_fit> #> Task:         multiclass #> Classes:      setosa, versicolor, virginica #> Formula:      Species ~ . #> Architecture: 4 -> 32 -> 32 -> 3 #> Fourier grids: 16  #> Epochs:       300 #> Batch size:   150 #> Validation:   no #> Standardize x: yes  #> Standardize y: no  #> Final train loss: 0.00819381 #> Best train loss:  0.00819381 at epoch 300 head(round(predict(fit_iris, iris, type = "prob"), 3)) #>      setosa versicolor virginica #> [1,]  0.999      0.001     0.000 #> [2,]  0.995      0.003     0.002 #> [3,]  0.997      0.001     0.001 #> [4,]  0.997      0.001     0.001 #> [5,]  0.999      0.000     0.000 #> [6,]  1.000      0.000     0.000 class_iris <- predict(fit_iris, iris, type = "class")  table(   observed = iris$Species,   predicted = class_iris ) #>             predicted #> observed     setosa versicolor virginica #>   setosa         50          0         0 #>   versicolor      0         50         0 #>   virginica       0          0        50

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: kaf_fit() supports validation splits, mini-batching, early stopping. fitted object stores training validation loss histories, users can inspect directly fit_val$train_loss_history fit_val$validation_loss_history. code shown example run building README.
code: fit_val <- kaf_fit(   x = x,   y = y,   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   use_layernorm = FALSE,   epochs = 300,   lr = 5e-4,   batch_size = 64,   validation_split = 0.2,   patience = 100,   restore_best = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE,   seed = 123 )  fit_val$train_loss_history fit_val$validation_loss_history

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: can inspect learned balance base/GELU branch Fourier branch.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: Advanced users can directly create KAF torch modules.
code: model <- nn_kaf(   layers = c(4, 16, 16, 1),   num_grids = 8 )  x_tensor <- torch::torch_randn(10, 4) y_tensor <- model(x_tensor)  y_tensor$shape #> [1] 10  1

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: package currently includes: full test coverage core API, including regression, binary classification, multiclass classification, formula handling, prediction, training utilities, diagnostics.
code: devtools::test() devtools::check()

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks in R
text: rkaf independent R implementation Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks using torch backend. package based KAF architecture proposed : Zhang, J., Fan, Y., Cai, K., & Wang, K. (2025). Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks. arXiv:2502.06018. implementation also informed public Python reference implementations: kolmogorovArnoldFourierNetwork/KAF kolmogorovArnoldFourierNetwork/kaf_act Python dependency required rkaf.

what: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
text: Convenience wrapper around nn_kaf() using user-facing API.

title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
code: kaf(   input_dim,   output_dim = 1,   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   activation_expectation = 1.64,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01 )

title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
text: input_dim Integer. Number input features. output_dim Integer. Number output dimensions. hidden Integer vector. Hidden layer sizes. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies per KAF layer. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant Fourier initialization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier branch.

title: Create a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf
text: torch KAF network.

what: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
text: Fits KAF model regression using mean squared error loss.

title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
code: kaf_fit(   x,   y,   task = c("auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass"),   hidden = c(64, 64),   num_grids = 16,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01,   epochs = 1000,   lr = 0.001,   batch_size = NULL,   shuffle = TRUE,   validation_split = 0,   x_val = NULL,   y_val = NULL,   weight_decay = 0,   standardize_x = TRUE,   standardize_y = NULL,   patience = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   print_every = 100,   seed = NULL,   restore_best = TRUE,   min_delta = 0 )

title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
text: x Matrix, data frame, vector, 2D torch tensor predictors. y Vector, matrix, data frame, torch tensor targets. task Character. One "auto", "regression", "binary", "multiclass". "auto", factor, character, logical targets treated classification; numeric targets treated regression. hidden Integer vector. Hidden layer sizes. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies per KAF layer. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier branch. epochs Integer. Maximum number training epochs. lr Numeric. Learning rate. batch_size Optional integer. Mini-batch size. NULL, full-batch training used. shuffle Logical. Whether shuffle training rows epoch. validation_split Numeric [0, 1). Fraction rows reserve validation. Ignored x_val y_val supplied. x_val Optional validation predictors. y_val Optional validation targets. weight_decay Numeric. Adam weight decay. standardize_x Logical. Whether standardize predictors using training-set mean standard deviation. standardize_y Logical NULL. Whether standardize regression targets using training-set mean standard deviation. NULL, targets standardized regression standardized classification. Predictions automatically transformed back original target scale. patience Optional integer. Number epochs without improvement early stopping. verbose Logical. Whether print progress. print_every Integer. Print frequency. seed Optional integer random seed. restore_best Logical. Whether restore best observed model state training. min_delta Numeric. Minimum loss improvement required update best model state.

title: Fit a Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — kaf_fit
text: object class "kaf_fit".

text: Fits Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network using formula data frame. convenience wrapper around kaf_fit() regression tasks.

title: Fit a KAF model using an R formula — kaf_fit_formula
code: kaf_fit_formula(   formula,   data,   include_intercept = FALSE,   na.action = stats::na.omit,   ... )

title: Fit a KAF model using an R formula — kaf_fit_formula
text: formula model formula, y ~ x1 + x2. data data frame. include_intercept Logical. Whether keep model-matrix intercept column. Defaults FALSE. na.action Missing-value handling function passed model.frame(). ... Additional arguments passed kaf_fit().

title: Fit a KAF model using an R formula — kaf_fit_formula

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
text: Torch module implementing stacked Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
code: nn_kaf(   layers,   num_grids = 8,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   activation_expectation = 1.64,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01 )

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
text: layers Integer vector. Network architecture, including input output dimensions. example, c(10, 64, 64, 1). num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies per KAF layer. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization Fourier feature block. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant Fourier initialization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier component.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Network — nn_kaf
text: torch nn_module.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: Torch module implementing one KAF layer using hybrid GELU trainable Random Fourier Features activation.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
code: nn_kaf_layer(   input_dim,   output_dim,   num_grids = 8,   dropout = 0,   use_layernorm = TRUE,   activation_expectation = 1.64,   fourier_init_scale = 0.01 )

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: input_dim Integer. Input dimension. output_dim Integer. Output dimension. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. use_layernorm Logical. Whether apply layer normalization Fourier feature block. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant Fourier initialization. fourier_init_scale Numeric. Initial scale Fourier component.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: torch nn_module.

title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Layer — nn_kaf_layer
text: layer computes feature-wise hybrid activation form: $$ z = \alpha \odot GELU(x) + \beta \odot RFF(x) $$ followed linear projection output dimension.

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
text: Torch module implementing trainable random Fourier features Kolmogorov-Arnold Fourier Networks.

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
code: nn_random_fourier_features(   input_dim,   num_grids = 8,   dropout = 0,   activation_expectation = 1.64 )

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
text: input_dim Integer. Input dimension. num_grids Integer. Number Fourier frequencies. dropout Numeric. Dropout probability. activation_expectation Numeric. Scaling constant initialization.

title: Random Fourier Features Layer — nn_random_fourier_features
text: torch nn_module.

title: Predict from a fitted KAF model — predict.kaf_fit
text: object fitted object returned kaf_fit(). newdata Matrix, data frame, vector, 2D torch tensor. type Character. Prediction type. "response" returns regression predictions regression, probabilities classification. "prob" returns probabilities classification. "class" returns predicted classes. "link" returns raw model logits/outputs. threshold Numeric. Classification threshold used binary class predictions. as_tensor Logical. TRUE, return torch tensor supported. ... Unused.
